read_plan.section: Seven Days After the First Casualty - Mediation Clerk
read_plan.preview: Pavel Ren had processed forty-two dispute filings before midday and rejected eleven for using war language where evidence belonged.

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Ashford is rougher than [[Valdenmoor]] in the way that port cities tend to be — more transient, more tolerant of the irregular, more oriented toward the transaction than the institution. [[Flux]] use here leans heavily t

The coastal territory north of Ashford — absorbed into [[Terravelle]] during the campaign in which Eddan's elder son died — has never fully settled into the kingdom's administration. The independent coastal communities t

Eddan considers Ashford one of the three non-negotiables of Terravellian security. Losing control of the eastern coast would cut off trade revenue that funds a significant portion of the kingdom's military and administra

Ashford's trade volume depends on three movement systems that overlap without merging cleanly. Bulk cargo arrives through the [[Maren Freight Chain]] and is redistributed through dock districts that can absorb schedule s

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

read_plan.preview: The first near-war grew out of the first generation of serious economic contact. Once the [[Equatorial Desert|desert crossing]] became viable on a regular basis, both kingdoms recognized that the eastern coastal shipping

read_plan.section: The Ruins Claim Incident (~95 Years Ago)
read_plan.preview: The second near-war was precipitated not by trade competition but by the [[Ancient Ruins|ruins research]] that Auralis had begun funding formally in the preceding decades. An Auralis-sponsored expedition reached what it

The third near-war was [[Expansion Faction|Expansion Faction]] policy made explicit. The Faction had been building influence in [[The Council of Auralis]] for a generation; the Ashford Blockade was its first major test o
Operators now track escalation through the [[Near-War Incident Ladder]], a practical model describing how routine enforcement events become strategic confrontation.
Border trade in Solumora describes regulated cross-kingdom movement of goods, records, and licensed carriers through inspection points such as [[Ashford]].
Border crossings operate through structured inspection protocols implemented by customs officials at designated crossing points.
Customs inspection is performed by guild-affiliated inspectors operating under kingdom authority.
[[Black Market Grimoire Circulation]] and other contraband movement exploit structural gaps in inspection capacity.
Cross-border trade between [[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] operates under layered regulatory systems administered by both crown authorities and guild councils.
Successful cross-border trade depends on established relationships connecting merchants, guild representatives, and administrative contacts on both sides of the border.
Cross-border transactions require layered documentation satisfying both kingdoms' requirements simultaneously.
Cross-border commerce concentrates on established routes with known inspection patterns and reliable infrastructure.
The **Near-War Incident Ladder** is the practical escalation pattern used by mid-level operators in [[Auralis]] and [[Terravelle]] to describe how routine border friction becomes strategic crisis.
Policy language treats incidents as isolated events. Operators handling shipments, couriers, and inspection reports see connected causality instead:
A customs office changes inspection weighting, paperwork format, or review thresholds. Officials describe this as normal compliance adjustment.
Constitutional disputes create unique pressure toward military posturing:

[[The Council of Auralis]] must demonstrate that emergency governance is legitimate and effective. The demonstration requires visible success: border security improves, rift threats decrease, population safety increases

[[Terravelle]] must demonstrate that [[Auralis]] constitutional breakdown represents genuine threat justifying defensive preparation rather than diplomatic overreaction exploiting internal [[Auralis]] politics.
Both kingdoms simultaneously trying to validate positions creates self-reinforcing escalation:

Current [[Flux]] research is dominated by institutional networks in both kingdoms, each organized around distinct philosophical priorities and strategic orientations. **Contemporary military preparation is increasingly r

**[[Crestward Flux Institute]]** pursues cost reduction, parallel execution patterns, and methods to achieve higher-tier effects through coordinated low-tier practitioner teams. Leadership: [[Casten Miel]] ([[Control Tie

**[[Northern Terravelle Academy]]** emphasizes practical settlement-scale application and coordinated research on [[Rift Incursions|rift mechanics]]. The Academy operates independently of state control (through funding d

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti

Cross-border crimes create jurisdictional problems neither kingdom can solve independently. A merchant defrauded in [[Auralis]] by a [[Terravelle]] practitioner cannot be compensated unless Terravelle cooperates. A fugit
Each kingdom maintains 3-4 coordination offices in border regions and major ports:
Border Coordination Offices cannot:

[[Auralis]] coordination offices operate formally. They document all requests, track case status systematically, and follow established procedures for information exchange. Requests are written, communication is official
